title: ACT runtime WWW-Authenticate validation contract (PRD-109)
description: Per PRD-109-R4, PRD-109-R5, PRD-109-R9. HTTP headers are not JSON, so this schema describes the structured shape PRD-600 (validator) and PRD-500 (consumer SDK) parse runtime 401 responses into. PRD-109-R5 mandates that a 401 response include one WWW-Authenticate header line per advertised scheme, in manifest auth.schemes order. The validator parses the headers and asserts this shape.

status_code.description: Per PRD-109-R4: 401 is reserved for 'authentication required to access this scope'. MUST NOT be used to distinguish 'exists but auth needed' from 'does not exist'.

www_authenticate.description: One challenge per advertised scheme, in manifest auth.schemes order. Per RFC 9110 §11.6.1, multiple challenges MAY be sent as separate header lines or comma-separated within one line; either form satisfies PRD-109. The validator normalizes both into this array.

scheme.description: RFC 9110 auth-scheme token (e.g., 'Basic', 'Bearer', 'OAuth'). MUST correspond 1:1 with one entry in manifest.auth.schemes.

params.description: auth-param tokens. For OAuth 2.0 challenges: 'realm' SHOULD be present; 'scope' SHOULD echo the scopes the resource requires. MUST NOT include user-identifying tokens, request-local nonces tied to a user, or any value that varies across identities for the same resource.

body.description: If a body is sent, it MUST conform to the error envelope (owned by PRD-100). PRD-109 imposes the constraint that error.code == 'auth_required' and error.message MUST NOT contain PII.
